headline: Quantinuum IPO Plan and Dividend Move Could Be A Game Changer For Honeywell International (HON)
summary: Honeywell International recently announced that its Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of US$1.19 per share, payable on June 5, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 15, 2026, and separately disclosed that Quantinuum has filed for a proposed IPO of its Class A common stock on the Nasdaq under the ticker “QNT.” The planned Quantinuum offering highlights how Honeywell’s exposure to quantum computing could reshape the financial and strategic relevance of its minority...

headline: Honeywell Investors Weigh Quantinuum IPO And Quantum Exposure Valuation
summary: Quantinuum, the quantum computing company in which Honeywell (NasdaqGS:HON) holds a significant stake, has filed for an IPO on the Nasdaq. The filing brings Honeywell's exposure to quantum technologies into sharper focus for public equity investors. Honeywell, best known for its industrial, aerospace, and building technologies, has been building a position in quantum computing through its stake in Quantinuum. With the IPO filing, that interest is now tied to a separate listed entity, giving...

headline: Assessing Honeywell International (HON) After The Planned Three-Way Breakup Announcement
summary: If you are wondering whether Honeywell International at around US$216 per share is offering good value today, the key is to look past the headline price and into what different valuation methods are actually saying. The stock has returned 0.8% over the last week, a 3.5% decline over the last 30 days, 10.3% year to date, 9.2% over 1 year, 25.4% over 3 years and 13.1% over 5 years. This gives you a mix of shorter term hesitation and longer term strength to weigh up. Recent coverage of...
